nodes.label: Alignment Before Implementation
nodes.rationale: Proposals create alignment before code is written because changing a document is cheaper than refactoring implementation.

nodes.label: Dynamic Command Loading
nodes.rationale: Command modules are dynamically imported to keep CLI startup fast.

nodes.label: Filesystem State Portability
nodes.rationale: Filesystem-based .squad state is required for workflows that must work beyond the CLI-only SQL tool.

nodes.label: Minimal Context Sharing
nodes.rationale: Delegations share only required capabilities, decisions, and handoff context to avoid leaking internal state and secrets.

nodes.label: Filesystem Is the Mesh
nodes.rationale: Agents retain a local file interface while git or HTTP only materializes remote state onto disk.

nodes.label: Phased Mesh Rollout
nodes.rationale: The mesh begins as a convention and advances only when project-level decisions justify added complexity.

nodes.label: Progressive Verdicting
nodes.rationale: Each scenario verdict is posted immediately so model connection loss cannot erase all accumulated results.

nodes.label: Explicit Preference Precedence
nodes.rationale: Economy mode changes only automatic Layer 3 selection because persistent config, session directives, and charter preferences express user intent.

nodes.label: Draft-Only Mode
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 requires a mandatory human review gate and prohibits autonomous posting.

nodes.label: Per-Operation Token Use
nodes.rationale: Reading a named account token without switching global state isolates one operation and avoids breaking parallel agents.

nodes.label: Parallel Worktrees
nodes.rationale: One worktree per simultaneous issue prevents filesystem collisions and branch-switching overhead.

nodes.label: Final Outcome Recording
nodes.rationale: Recording confirmed outcomes rather than intermediate requests prevents stale history from poisoning later decisions.

nodes.label: Roster Confirmation Gate
nodes.rationale: No team files are created until the user explicitly confirms the proposed roster.

nodes.label: Members Header Contract
nodes.rationale: The exact Members heading is required because GitHub workflows use it for label automation.

nodes.label: Union Merge Driver for Squad State
Research Then Execute
nodes.rationale: Research and analysis must end with a concrete action or explicit coordinator sign-off to be considered complete.

nodes.label: Persistent Model Preferences
nodes.rationale: Persistent Layer 0 configuration wins because it records the user's explicit quality and cost choice.
